---
title: "Project Setup"
subtitle: "RProjects, renv, and Folder Structure"
margin-footer: |
  <div style="text-align: center; margin-bottom: 1em;">
    <a href="../slides/01_setup.html" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm" target="_blank">
      📊 Open slides ↗
    </a>
  </div>
  <div style="text-align: center;">
    <a href="https://www.leibniz-zas.de/en/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" style="display: inline-block; margin-right: 20px;">
      <img src="/logos/ZAS_Marke-Kurz_4c.png" alt="ZAS" style="height: 70px; width: auto;"/>
    </a>
    <a href="https://www.leibniz-gemeinschaft.de/en/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" style="display: inline-block;">
      <img src="/logos/Leibniz-Gemeinschaft-Logo-ENG_blau.png" alt="Leibniz" style="height: 60px; width: auto;"/>
    </a>
  </div>
---

```{r}
#| output: false
library(here)
library(renv)
```

## Before you start

Before the first session, please make sure you have the following installed:

- **R** (≥ 4.2): <https://cran.r-project.org>
- **RStudio** (≥ 2022.07): <https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop>
- **Quarto** (latest): <https://quarto.org/docs/download>

### Checking your versions

In the R console:
```{r}
#| output: false
R.version.string  # R version
```

To check your Quarto CLI version, run this in the **Terminal** tab:
```bash
quarto --version
```

RStudio's version is visible under *Help → About RStudio*.

### Updating

- **R**: download the latest version from <https://cran.r-project.org>
- **RStudio**: *Help → Check for Updates*
- **Quarto**: download the latest version from <https://quarto.org/docs/download>


## Why project hygiene matters

Reproducibility begins before you write a single line of analysis. A clean, self-contained project structure ensures that collaborators — and your future self — can re-run everything without hunting for files or reinstalling packages.

## RProjects

Always work inside an RProject (`.Rproj`). This sets the working directory to the project root and makes `here::here()` reliable.

### Creating a new RProject

In RStudio: *File → New Project → New Directory → New Project*. Tick "Use renv with this project" to initialise package management from the start.

In Positron: *File → New Folder → initialise as R project*.

## Folder structure

A minimal reproducible structure would look something like:

```
my-project/
├── my-project.Rproj
├── _quarto.yml         # if using Quarto
├── data/
│   ├── raw/            # original data, never modified
│   └── processed/      # cleaned/transformed outputs
├── manuscript/         # .qmd chapters
└── output/
    ├── figures/
    └── tables/
```

Importantly, you'd have a `data/` folder with subfolders separating your `raw/` data from the `processed/` data. You would never want to alter the `raw/` data.

:::{.callout-warning}
## Data protection: raw and identifiable data

If your project involves personal, sensitive, or identifiable data (e.g., 
recordings, transcripts, demographic information), keep the following in mind:

- **Never commit raw data to version control.** Add `data/raw/` to your 
  `.gitignore` before your first commit. This prevents raw data from ever being tracked accidentally.
- **Never share the project folder** (e.g., as a zip, via GitHub, or cloud 
  sync) without first checking what `data/raw/` contains.
- **Pseudonymisation/anonymisation comes first.** Only once raw data has been 
  processed and (pseudo)anonymised should derived outputs be treated as 
  shareable.
- **Archive, then remove.** Ideally, raw data lives in `data/raw/` only during 
  active processing. Once archived to a secure location (e.g., the NAS), 
  remove it from the project folder.

The `data/processed/` folder should contain only anonymised or 
non-identifiable outputs.

:::

## Project-relative file paths

Use `here::here()` for all file paths so the project is portable:

```{r}
#| eval: false
data_raw <- readr::read_csv(here::here("data", "raw", "mydata.csv"))
```

## renv: reproducible package management

`renv` records the exact version of every package your project uses in a lockfile (`renv.lock`). Anyone who clones your project and runs `renv::restore()` gets an identical environment.

```{r}
#| eval: false
renv::init()       # initialise (done once at project start)
renv::snapshot()   # update lockfile after installing new packages
renv::restore()    # recreate environment from lockfile
```

::: callout-warning
`renv` solves the "works on my machine" problem but has limits over long time horizons — R and system library changes can still break things. Document your R version in a `README.md`.
:::

## Quarto in an RProject

If your project produces a Quarto book or report, place `_quarto.yml` at the project root. Quarto and RStudio/Positron detect it automatically.

### Chapter slides {.slides-link}

```{=html}
<a href="../slides/01_setup.html" class="btn btn-outline-primary" target="_blank">
  Open slides ↗
</a>
```
